title: Share of the population with bipolar disorder, by age
subtitle: Estimated share of people who have  [bipolar disorder](#dod:bipolar_disorder), whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.
caption:
Share of the population with bipolar disorder, by age

Estimated share of people who have  bipolar disorder, whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.

This horizontal bar chart displays the estimated share of the global population with bipolar disorder in 2023, categorized by specific age groups. The data, derived from representative surveys, medical data, and statistical modelling, presents values as percentages. The age group "Ages 5-14" shows the lowest prevalence at 0.04%, represented by the shortest bar at the bottom of the chart. Moving upward, the "Ages 15-19" group sits at 0.42%, a value shared by the "70+ years" category and the "Age-standardized" metric. The "All ages" average is recorded at 0.44%. The highest shares are concentrated in the middle age brackets. Specifically, "Ages 40-44" and "Ages 45-49" both register at 0.66%, forming the longest bars in the visualization. Surrounding these peaks, several groups cluster in the 0.60% to 0.64% range: "Ages 50-54" is at 0.64%, while "Ages 55-59", "Ages 60-64", "Ages 35-39", and "Ages 25-29" all sit at 0.62%. The "Ages 30-34" group is slightly lower at 0.61%. The "Ages 20-24" and "Ages 65-69" categories both show a share of 0.58% and 0.57% respectively. The visual arrangement highlights a rise in prevalence from adolescence into early adulthood, a plateau through middle age, and a gradual decline in the older age groups, returning to lower levels comparable to the age-standardized rate by the 70+ category.
